| **Doctor In The House** | Welcome back to St. Swithin’s! The misadventures of the medical students of St.<br>Swithin’s Hospital were first introduced to the public in 1952 with the<br>publication of Richard Gordon’s comic novel DOCTOR IN THE HOUSE. Inspired by his<br>own time as a student at Cambridge University and St Bartholemew’s Hospital in<br>the 1940s, Gordon’s tale was written during an idle period at sea while working<br>as a ship’s surgeon. “I had nothing to do except drink gin with the chief<br>engineer,” he later explained. “To save myself from developing cirrhosis of the<br>liver, I wrote about my experiences as a medical student.” The novel was a great<br>success, selling over three million copies and being translated into nearly<br>twenty languages. Over the next thirty years, Richard Gordon wrote a further<br>seventeen novels in the DOCTOR series. He also wrote a number of non-fiction<br>books, a textbook on anaesthesiology, and was a regular contributor to Punch<br>magazine. The success of DOCTOR IN THE HOUSE soon attracted the attention of the<br>Rank Organisation. The film adaptation, starring Dirk Bogarde as naïve medical<br>student Simon Sparrow and James Robertson Justice as irascible surgeon Sir<br>Lancelot Spratt, was the number one movie at the British box office in 1954.<br>Five sequels followed; DOCTOR AT SEA (1955), DOCTOR AT LARGE (1957), DOCTOR IN<br>LOVE (1960), DOCTOR IN DISTRESS (1963), DOCTOR IN CLOVER (1966), and DOCTOR IN<br>TROUBLE (1970). A popular stage adaptation of DOCTOR IN THE HOUSE was first<br>performed in 1956 and has been a regular attraction for many professional and<br>amateur theatre companies ever since. A 13-episode BBC radio adaptation of<br>DOCTOR IN THE HOUSE also appeared in 1968, starring Richard Briers as Simon<br>Sparrow and Geoffrey Sumner as Sir Lancelot Spratt. DOCTOR AT LARGE followed for<br>a further 13 episodes the following year. In 1969, LWT launched a new television<br>adaptation of DOCTOR IN THE HOUSE with scripts by John Cleese and Graham<br>Chapman, Bill Oddie and Graeme Garden, and Barry Cryer. The series saw the<br>characters and situations updated for a more liberal age. Barry Evans starred as<br>student Michael Upton, alongside Robin Nedwell as Duncan Warning, Geoffrey<br>Davies as Dick Stuart-Clark, George Layton as Paul Collier, and Ernest Clark as<br>Professor Loftus. DOCTOR IN THE HOUSE ran for 26 episodes over two hugely<br>popular series. It was followed by DOCTOR IN LARGE (29 episodes, 1971), DOCTOR<br>IN CHARGE (43 episodes, 1972-73), DOCTOR AT SEA (13 episodes, 1974), and DOCTOR<br>ON THE GO (26 episodes, 1975-77). Further sequels followed with DOCTOR DOWN<br>UNDER in Australia (13 episodes, 1979) and a switch of channels for DOCTOR AT<br>THE TOP on BBC1 (7 episodes, 1991). Now we are returning to St. Swithin’s!<br>DOCTOR IN THE HOUSE is a brand new audio sitcom updating Richard Gordon’s<br>classic story of misbehaving medical students for a new generation. Reflecting<br>the changes in medical education and the daily lives of students, and tackling<br>wider issues within the NHS and beyond, this modern adaptation is in keeping<br>with the spirit of the original with a contemporary edge. The first series<br>features six 40-minute episodes. The first episode is released on Sunday 20th<br>December 2025 with a new episode each week. Written and produced by Daniel Ward,<br>DOCTOR IN THE HOUSE stars Will Trafford, Alex White, Emily Cass, Louis Suc,<br>Vinay Lad, and Virginia Byron, with Jim Burke, Jacob Hulland, Sally Swift,<br>Christie-Leigh Emby, Patrick Hibbin, Nathan Luwa, Caitlin Wood, Anusia<br>Battersby, Tom Williams, and Michael Everhard. The theme music is composed by<br>Alan Tew, and arranged and performed by James Rose. DOCTOR IN THE HOUSE is an<br>OnlyAudio Production. Hosted on Acast. See for more information. | [↗](https://open.spotify.com/show/4m39b2bpKqIU5w5uvAtamZ) |

Tìm hiểu<br>cụ thể lập trình di độngLập trình ứng dụng di động hay còn gọi là App Mobile,<br>đây là một thuật ngữ được dùng để chỉ những người làm công việc liên quan đến<br>thiết kế, xây dựng, phát triển các ứng dụng phần mềm trên điện thoại di động.<br>Mỗi một chương trình được viết ra sẽ có những chức năng riêng biệt để đáp ứng<br>mục đích sử dụng tốt nhất cho người dùng. Các ứng dụng được thiết kế ra có thể<br>được dùng để phục vụ việc học tập, giải trí hoặc mua sắm Online, trò chuyện với<br>bạn bè,...Lập trình di động đang dần phát triển mạnh trên toàn Thế GiớiĐể viết<br>nên những ứng dụng di động, trong quá trình lập trình các lập trình viên sẽ phải<br>sử dụng đến sự trợ giúp của các ngôn ngữ lập trình chuyên dụng và các công cụ<br>thiết kế khác. Việc sử dụng các ngôn ngữ sẽ giúp cho các nhà thiết kế dễ dàng<br>dàng tạo chương trình để thỏa mãn nhu cầu sử dụng ở mọi phương diện của người<br>dùng. 2. Những điều cần chú ý sử dụng ngôn ngữ lập trình ứng dụng di độngHiện<br>nay, lập trình mobile app được phát triển chủ yếu trên hai nền tảng hệ điều hành<br>chính, đó là: Android phần mềm được cung ứng trên App CH Play và hệ điều hành<br>IOS phân phối các chương trình ứng dụng trên App Store. Mỗi một nền tảng sẽ có<br>một ngôn ngữ lập trình riêng phù hợp để phục vụ tốt nhất trong quá trình tạo lập<br>chương trình của các lập trình viên.2.1 Ngôn ngữ lập trình hệ điều hành<br>AndroidĐối với hệ điều hành Android, thông thường các ngôn ngữ lập trình được sử<br>dụng phổ biến gồm có 2 ngôn ngữ là:Hệ điều hành Android sử dụng hai ngôn ngữ cơ<br>bản Java & KotlinNgôn ngữ JAVA: ngôn ngữ Java là ngôn ngữ được sử dụng phổ biến<br>trong các thiết kế ứng dụng Web và di động. Đây cũng là ngôn ngữ chính dùng cho<br>hệ điều hành Android. Bên cạnh đó, ngôn ngữ lập trình còn được sử dụng trong<br>việc hỗ trợ các ứng dụng di động khác. Nếu bạn học ngôn ngữ này làm nền tảng sẽ<br>rất tốt cho việc bạn sử dụng các ngôn ngữ khác một cách dễ dàng hơn.Ngôn ngữ<br>Kotlin: là ngôn ngữ được các lập trình viên ưu tiên hàng đầu khi xây dựng ứng<br>dụng để vận hành trên Android. Kotlin là một ngôn ngữ được đánh giá là khá dễ<br>học và dễ dàng tương tác hơn, nhờ vào tính năng đơn giản, ngắn gọn. Kotlin được<br>các nhà phát triển khoa học sáng tạo ra nhằm mục đích giải quyết tất cả những<br>hạn chế khi làm việc với JAVA.2.2 Ngôn ngữ lập trình ứng dụng trong hệ điều hành<br>IOSIOS là một hệ điều hành lớn được sử dụng nhiều nhất, các ứng dụng lập trình<br>di động trên hệ điều hành IOS thường được các lập trình viên sử dụng các ngôn<br>ngữ như:Ngôn ngữ Swift đang được sử dụng chính trong việc tạo ứng dụng trên hệ<br>điều hành IOSNgôn ngữ Swift: Ngôn ngữ lập trình Swift là một trong những ngôn<br>ngữ được Apple sáng tạo để phục vụ cho quá trình xây dựng và phát triển ứng dụng<br>di động vận hành trên hệ điều hành độc quyền của hãng. Phần lớn Swift là ngôn<br>ngữ lập trình được các lập trình viên sử dụng chính để phát triển các ứng dụng<br>IOS. Bởi tính năng sử dụng đơn giản, ngắn gọn và nhanh chóng, phù hợp với các<br>Syntax để phát hiện các lỗi khi viết App Mobile.Ngôn ngữ Objective - C: Ngôn ngữ<br>Objective là ngôn ngữ được sử dụng đầu tiên trong việc tạo ra các ứng dụng tiện<br>ích được dùng để vận hành trên IOS. Khi sử dụng ngôn ngữ này các lập viên sẽ cảm<br>thấy dễ dàng hơn trong việc thực hiện các công việc liên quan. Từ xây dựng ý<br>tưởng Common Line Utilities cho đến việc thiết kế giao diện cho người dùng bằng<br>đồ họa hoặc các thư viện.3. Học lập trình di động ở đâu uy tín?Lập trình di động<br>đang là một trong những ngành học hót nhất hiện nay được đông đảo giới trẻ yêu<br>thích. Do đó mà có rất nhiều trung tâm, cơ sở nhận dạy và đào tạo các lập trình<br>viên. Tuy nhiên để đánh giá thì FTP Aptech là một trong những đơn vị đào tạo<br>chất lượng được rất nhiều bạn trẻ lựa chọn. Rất hiếm để thấy được có một đơn vị<br>nào chương trình giảng dạy phần lớn là các kiến thức, kỹ năng được áp dụng thực<br>hành nhiều. Khi học tại FTP Aptech các học viên sẽ được tiếp cận với các đội ngũ<br>giảng viên, kỹ sư đầu ngành về công nghệ thông tin, được truyền tải những kinh<br>nghiệm quý giá trong thực tiễn để các học viên có thêm những kỹ năng khi ra<br>trường làm việc. Đặc biệt, khi ra trường bạn sẽ nhận được tấm bằng chứng chỉ Lập<br>trình viên Quốc Tế được các nhà tuyển dụng liên kết với Aptech chào đón.Lời<br>kếtTrên đây là những thông tin viết về Lập trình di động và những điều cần chú ý<br>để trở thành một lập trình viên di động. Nếu bạn đang quan tâm đến các khóa học<br>lập trình di động hoặc các ngành lập trình web thì hãy nhanh tay đến Hệ thống<br>đào tạo lập trình viên quốc tế Aptech để tìm hiểu và được hướng dẫn cụ thể trong<br>định hướng ngành học. | [↗](https://open.spotify.com/show/2IotzSmoaqfUOKMGBiVOgH) |

| **Mobile Development with Fexingo: iOS, Android, and App Building Conversations** | Lucas and Luna explore the craft of building mobile apps, from iOS and Android<br>fundamentals to architecture decisions and deployment workflows. Each episode<br>digs into a single practical question: how to structure a feature, manage state<br>across platforms, or optimize for performance without sacrificing readability.<br>The hosts debate real-world trade-offs using concrete examples—a navigation<br>pattern in SwiftUI versus Jetpack Compose, the role of dependency injection in<br>testable code, or when to reach for a cross-platform framework. They avoid hype<br>and focus on what works in production, citing open-source libraries and<br>documented case studies from companies like Airbnb, Spotify, and Basecamp. Lucas<br>brings a journalist's rigor, asking why a team chose one approach over another;<br>Luna pushes back with hands-on nuance, drawing from her own experience shipping<br>apps. Together, they serve engineers, technical leads, and product managers who<br>want to stay sharp without chasing every new tool. The conversation assumes you<br>already know the basics and are looking for deeper reasoning—not tutorials. By<br>the end of each episode, you'll have a clearer sense of how to evaluate<br>trade-offs in your own codebase. What does it really take to build an app that<br>users love and teams can maintain? #MobileDevelopment #IOS #Android #AppBuilding<br>#SwiftUI #JetpackCompose #CrossPlatform #SoftwareArchitecture #MobileEngineering<br>#StateManagement #DependencyInjection #AppPerformance #CodeQuality #TechPodcast<br>#Technology #Business #FexingoBusiness #BusinessPodcast Keep every episode free: | [↗](https://open.spotify.com/show/033kZieaIfT54REdiBkaYX) |

| **Compose** | Compose is an interview podcast for software developers using the Rust<br>programming language to build a better planet. Software's impact on our society<br>is increasing every day. Let's pull things apart so we can learn how to put<br>things back together. Interview guests are typically the maintainers of open<br>source projects, startup founders, leaders within the technology industry, and<br>the occasional critic. Each episode aims to teach something new and provoke<br>thoughts that'll last until the next episode and beyond. Interviews follow a<br>rough format. We start with something that our guest thinks is interesting. This<br>will typically involve talking tech and/or recent events. This section means<br>that you'll find something to geek out about within the first 5-10mins of every<br>episode. Then we learn more about our guest(s) by discussing their recent<br>background and how they came to be interested in the thing they're interested<br>in. At this point, we've really started to gain an understanding of their<br>personal system at play and the forces that are upon it. We'll try to expand on<br>some of those forces. While every interview is unique, some of the themes<br>include the pressures of software engineering within a team, security, creating<br>open source technology, the growth of the Rust language, and trends within the<br>software industry. Compose is hosted by Tim Clicks, also known as Tim McNamara.<br>Tim is an avid supporter of Rust, and continues to advocate for the language and<br>its promise of reliable, efficient software. | [↗](https://open.spotify.com/show/7D949LgDm36qaSq32IObI0) |

| **EH-Introduction_to_Ethical_Hacking** | An ethical hacker uses hacking techniques to assess and improve the security of<br>computer systems and networks with permission from the owner. They simulate<br>real-world attacks to identify vulnerabilities and weaknesses before malicious<br>actors can exploit them. The key distinction between an ethical hacker and a<br>malicious hacker is permission and intent. | **The OPSEC Podcast** | Welcome to The OPSEC Podcast - where operational security meets everyday<br>life.I'm your host, Allen P. - former Navy aircrew, defense contractor, and<br>cybersecurity professional with over 15 years of international intelligence<br>operations experience. From the back of military aircraft to Intelligence<br>Community-contracted programs, from Cyber Command to corporate security - I've<br>seen what's possible when privacy and security break down.But here's the thing:<br>nobody's coming to save you. The companies won't fix this for you. The<br>government won't protect your privacy. Your security is your<br>responsibility.Every two weeks, we'll dive deep into the world of operational<br>security - not just as a professional practice, but as a way of life. We'll<br>cover signature reduction, security operations, privacy strategies, and the<br>OPSEC mindset that can protect you whether you're an intelligence professional,<br>a corporate analyst, or someone who simply values their privacy and<br>security.From digital tools and daily carry items to situational awareness and<br>travel security - this is practical, actionable intelligence you can use today.<br>Because in a world where your data is currency and your privacy is under<br>constant attack, the best defense is being your own first line of<br>security.Strong body, strong mind. Be the leader of your tribe. Welcome to The<br>OPSEC Podcast. Hosted on Acast. See for more information. | [↗](https://open.spotify.com/show/4FP7QYrlhj92eTRwcoe8Fy) |

| **Anatomy of an End** | Why do people fall through the cracks, and how do we stop the cycle?Welcome to<br>Anatomy of an End, a raw, honest, and uncompromised look at suicide prevention,<br>mental health advocacy, and the systemic failures of our emergency response<br>infrastructure. Hosted by a citizen advocate and a seasoned clinician and<br>forensic psychological consultant, this podcast strips away the sanitized<br>scripts and societal taboos to look directly at the mechanics of crisis.Each<br>episode uses the framework of a psychological autopsy to dissect real case<br>studies. We examine the invisible behavioral patterns, personal shame, and<br>institutional voids that lead to a tragic end. But we don't stop at the autopsy.<br>Our mission is to translate profound loss into actionable prevention.By bringing<br>the heavy, unspoken realities of depression, isolation, and psychological trauma<br>into a casual, frank, and approachable space, we are building an army of allies.<br>We are shifting the burden of prevention off the shoulders of loss survivors and<br>putting it back into the social zeitgeist.Because society is the ultimate front<br>line.New episodes drop weekly. Hit follow on Spotify, Apple Podcasts, or your<br>favorite directory to join the room. | [↗](https://open.spotify.com/show/033cLJnyR1daqUKuN0Hqky) |

| **Certified - AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner Audio Course** | Ready to earn your AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner credential? The AWS Cloud<br>Practitioner Audio Course is your complete, audio-first companion for mastering<br>the foundational knowledge required to succeed on the certification exam.<br>Designed for learners at all levels, this Audio Course breaks down the<br>essentials of the Amazon Web Services (AWS) Cloud into clear, practical, and<br>easy-to-follow lessons. Each episode covers key topics including cloud concepts,<br>AWS core services, security and compliance, cost management, and cloud<br>economics—all aligned with the official exam blueprint. Whether you’re brand new<br>to IT or expanding your cloud expertise, you’ll gain the understanding and<br>confidence to speak the language of the cloud and apply these principles in<br>real-world environments. The AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner certification<br>validates a broad understanding of cloud computing and the AWS platform, making<br>it ideal for students, business professionals, and technical teams seeking<br>foundational cloud fluency. The exam measures your ability to explain AWS<br>architectural principles, describe key security and compliance measures, and<br>identify use cases that drive business value. It also emphasizes cost<br>optimization and pricing models, preparing you to make informed cloud decisions<br>in both technical and non-technical roles. Earning this certification is an<br>essential first step toward advanced AWS credentials like Solutions Architect,<br>SysOps Administrator, or Security Specialty. Developed by the AWS Cloud<br>Practitioner Audio Course delivers expert-driven instruction designed for<br>flexible, on-the-go learning. Each episode builds your cloud literacy and exam<br>readiness—helping you confidently prepare, pass, and take the next step in your<br>cloud career. | [↗](https://open.spotify.com/show/3Kfpi2wm8bjRVL5YEV6asV) |
| **Certified - The CompTIA A+ 1201-1202 &#124; V15 Audio Course** | Certified: The CompTIA A+ Audio Course is built for people who want a practical,<br>spoken path into entry-level IT support without wasting time on filler. It fits<br>career changers, help desk candidates, junior technicians, students, and working<br>adults who need to study during a commute, a walk, or between real<br>responsibilities. The course assumes you are serious about learning the<br>fundamentals, but it does not assume years of technical experience. It starts<br>where many learners actually are: interested in IT, motivated to get certified,<br>and looking for a resource that explains things plainly. If you have felt buried<br>by dense books, scattered videos, or study materials that jump too quickly from<br>topic to topic, this course is designed to feel more direct and easier to<br>follow. It gives you a steady way to build understanding while staying focused<br>on the real knowledge areas that matter for CompTIA A+. This course teaches the<br>core subjects people expect from A+, including hardware, mobile devices,<br>networking, operating systems, security, troubleshooting, and operational<br>procedures. More important, it explains how those topics connect in real support<br>work, so you are not just memorizing terms and hoping they stick. Each episode<br>is written for listening first, which means the lessons are structured to make<br>sense without slides, labs, or printed handouts in front of you. Complex ideas<br>are broken into clear spoken steps, and technical terms are introduced in a way<br>that helps you keep moving instead of getting lost. That makes the course useful<br>for repeat listening, which is one of the biggest advantages of audio. You can<br>reinforce key concepts while driving, exercising, doing chores, or getting ready<br>for work, turning small blocks of time into steady exam preparation. What makes<br>this course different is that it respects both the certification and the<br>listener. It does not try to sound flashy, and it does not bury the point under<br>jargon. Instead, it treats A+ as a practical foundation for real IT work and<br>teaches the material in a calm, credible voice that helps you stay engaged over<br>time. The goal is not just to help you recognize exam language, but to help you<br>understand what a technician is seeing, deciding, and fixing. Success here means<br>you can hear a support scenario, identify the likely issue, think through the<br>right tools or settings, and make a sound choice with confidence. By the end,<br>you should feel more prepared for the exam, more fluent in the language of IT<br>support, and more ready to step into entry-level technical work with a solid<br>base under you. | [↗](https://open.spotify.com/show/1SHEjCwjdDO05q3NdkeWbz) |

| **Energy Gang** | Covering breaking news in clean tech, going deep on global energy policy, and<br>debating the levers that need to move to accelerate the energy transition.<br>Energy Gang is the podcast covering clean energy technology, renewable energy,<br>and the environment. You’ll find it on Wood<br>Mackenzie’s Energy Gang.How will changes to the US government affect<br>decarbonisation and energy security? When will hydrogen, nuclear and carbon<br>capture deploy at scale? Where’s the money for the energy transition green<br>finance coming from and how much more is needed? What’s the outlook for EVs?<br>What are the energy predictions for solar energy? What's the latest on climate<br>change?Get answers to questions like these, bi-weekly on Tuesdays at 7am ET.<br>Plus, get special live episodes recorded at the biggest climate and energy<br>events throughout the year, like COP30 and Climate Week NYC. Don’t worry if you<br>can’t make it in person, Energy Gang brings you all the updates on energy<br>policy, energy finance and energy innovation you need to hear.Energy Gang is<br>presented by Wood Mackenzie and hosted by Ed Crooks, Vice-Chairman of Energy at<br>Wood Mackenzie and a former Financial Times and BBC News journalist. Regular<br>guests are Amy Myers-Jaffe (Director of NYU’s Energy, Climate Justice and<br>Sustainability Lab), and Dr Melissa Lott (Partner at Microsoft) – plus a roster<br>of industry leaders and policy influencers, like Jigar Shah (Industry figurehead<br>and former director of the Loan Programs Office in the US Department of Energy),<br>Caroline Golin (Head of North America, Global Energy Market Development and<br>Policy at Google) and Ambassador Geoffrey Pyatt (Former Assistant Secretary of<br>State for Energy Resources).If you like The Energy Transition Show,<br>Catalyst with Shayle Kann, The Big Switch from Columbia University, Open<br>Circuit with Stephen Lacey or The Green Blueprint, you’ll enjoy Energy Gang.Want<br>to get involved with the show? | **Growth Mindset Psychology: The Science of Self-Improvement** | Most self-help advice is guesswork dressed up as wisdom. We dig into psychology,<br>neuroscience, and behavioral science to find what actually changes lives. WHY<br>LISTEN? Over 8 million downloads because we answer the questions that matter:<br>How do I build mental strength? What creates lasting motivation? How can I<br>understand my own mind well enough to work with it, not against it? This podcast<br>is for curious skeptics who want frameworks backed by psychology studies, not<br>Instagram quotes. Whether you're navigating procrastination, building<br>self-discipline, or designing your own philosophy for a life well lived—we<br>explore the hidden psychology behind real change. WHAT YOU'LL GET Mental models<br>and simple techniques from cognitive psychology, positive psychology, and<br>ancient wisdom traditions like Stoicism and Buddhism. Being a human can be<br>confusing, learning about it doesn’t have to be. We cover everything from<br>emotional intelligence and habit formation to brain health and the nervous<br>system. WHAT YOU DON’T GET No pseudoscience. No oversimplified "hacks." No<br>hustle porn. Just practical insights on mindset improvement, self-development,<br>and human psychology that respect your intelligence and mental health. THE<br>APPROACH Instead of telling you what to think, we explore how thinking works.<br>Armed with psychology studies, social science research, and relentless<br>curiosity, we uncover the mechanics of belief change, attitude change, and<br>personal development. Success is personal. You might want to leverage your<br>neurodiverse strengths, build a business, or simply discover how to be happier.<br>We provide the mental frameworks to pursue your definition of success with<br>mental strength and self-care that fits your life. YOUR HOST I'm Sam Webster<br>Harris—a lifelong learner with ADHD and an obsession with finding answers to<br>hard questions. After launching businesses, traveling the world, and nearly<br>dying a few times, I concluded that psychology and science are where real wisdom<br>lives. This show is my excuse to dive deep into health and fitness research,<br>behavioral psychology, and cool science while helping you build genuine<br>self-discipline and motivation. PREMIUM Go ad-free and access exclusive content,<br>AMAs, and our community Discord. Growth Mindset Premium PAST SERIES Previous<br>guests include Olympians, Scientists, Billionaires, and Sam's Mum. Past series:<br>— Psychology vs Stoicism — Time Management for Busy Mortals — Independence and<br>Knowing Yourself — Cognitive Biases and Rational Thinking — Psychology of<br>Connection and Relationships — Carol Dweck and the Multiverse of Mindsets —<br>Addiction and Behaviour Change — Nervous System Mastery and Polyvagal Theory —<br>Mental Strength and Self-Discipline | [↗](https://open.spotify.com/show/2rQ6Aug8EBudJMSBYTi8nM) |

| **カピぞうの寝言 &#124; HTMLとCSSとWEBデザインと。** | HTML・CSS・Webデザインを学習中の方が楽しく学ぶための作業BGMみたいなポッドキャスト。<br>現役フリーランスのWeb制作者であり書籍著者が現場の話やどうでもいい雑談をゆるく語ります。 累計6万4千部を突破した『これだけで基本がしっかり身につく<br>HTML/CSS&Webデザイン1冊目の本』（通称：カピバラ本）では「正しく、わかりやすく」を心がけて解説していますが、ここでは一人のWeb制作者に戻って台本なしのトークをお届けします。<br>HTML・CSSを学習中の方に向けた学び方やマインドについてを中心に よくある質問に対する答えや最新技術トレンド、 役立つか分からない失敗談や完全な雑談まで。<br>聴くと少しだけWebが楽しくなるかもしれない話を、リラックスした雰囲気でお話しします。 勉強のお供に、通勤のBGMに、あるいは寝る前のひとときに。<br>カピバラの寝言に耳を傾けてみませんか？ 【こんなことを話します】 学習のヒント：無駄の少ない勉強の仕方や伸びる人の特徴<br>技術のヒント：HTML/CSSのちょっとしたコツ 技術トレンド：気になっている新技術やツール 制作裏話：失敗談や、カピバラ本の制作秘話<br>完全な雑談：最近食べたものや、散歩中に考えたこと 【こんな人におすすめ】 「カピバラ本」で勉強中の方 Webデザイン・制作に興味がある方<br>作業中に聴ける、ゆるい技術トークを探している方 | [↗](https://open.spotify.com/show/1uzai0TUDsQS9ZPcSXfrEw) |
